For AV Engineers\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nWatch or Listen on YouTube\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nAlpamayo-R1 Review: From Black Box to Glass Box\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Introduction\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nIf you have ever watched a self-driving demo and thought, “Cool, but why did it do that?”, you are not alone. Autonomous driving has spent a decade getting better at perception, better at prediction, and better at path planning, while staying stubbornly bad at explaining itself. When something goes weird, the stack usually responds with silence, or worse, a confidence score.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n[Alpamayo-R1](https://developer.nvidia.com/drive/alpamayo) is [NVIDIA’s](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/solutions/autonomous-vehicles/alpamayo/) attempt to make driving policies less opaque. Not by bolting a chatbot on top of a planner, but by training a single vision-language-action model that can both talk through a decision and output a trajectory you can execute. Following the release of NVIDIA Alpamayo at CES 2026 (NVIDIA, [2026](https://arxiv.org/html/2511.00088v2#bib.bib65)), Alpamayo-R1 is also referred to as Alpamayo 1.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n###### Abstract\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nEnd-to-end architectures trained via imitation learning have advanced autonomous driving by scaling model size and data, yet performance remains brittle in safety-critical long-tail scenarios where supervision is sparse and causal understanding is limited. We introduce Alpamayo-R1 (AR1), a vision–language–action model (VLA) that integrates Chain of Causation reasoning with trajectory planning for complex driving scenarios. Autonomous driving has spent a decade getting better at perception, better at prediction, and better at path planning, while staying stubbornly bad at explaining itself. When something goes weird, the stack usually responds with silence, or worse, a confidence score.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n[Alpamayo-R1](https://developer.nvidia.com/drive/alpamayo) is [NVIDIA’s](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/solutions/autonomous-vehicles/alpamayo/) attempt to make driving policies less opaque. Not by bolting a chatbot on top of a planner, but by training a single vision-language-action model that can both talk through a decision and output a trajectory you can execute. When something goes weird, the stack usually responds with silence, or worse, a confidence score.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n[Alpamayo-R1](https://developer.nvidia.com/drive/alpamayo) is [NVIDIA’s](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/solutions/autonomous-vehicles/alpamayo/) attempt to make driving policies less opaque. Not by bolting a chatbot on top of a planner, but by training a single vision-language-action model that can both talk through a decision and output a trajectory you can execute. VLM 的 vocab 被扩展了（添加了轨迹相关的 special tokens）\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n3. 推理分两阶段：第一阶段是 VLM 自回归生成 CoT 文本 tokens（用 model.generate()），第二阶段是用 VLM 的 KV cache (past_key_values) 喂给一个单独的 expert model 做 diffusion denoising\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n4. 第二阶段的 expert model 需要访问第一阶段的 KV cache\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n请搜索以下内容：\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n1. vLLM 是否支持 Qwen3-VL（Qwen2.5-VL 或 Qwen3-VL）的推理？如何集成？\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n2. TensorRT-LLM 是否支持 Qwen3-VL？\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n3. 这两种方案中，是否能获取到中间的 KV cache 用于后续的 expert model？\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n4. 是否有其他更轻量的加速方案（如 torch.compile, SGLang 等）？\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n重点关注 2025-2026 年的最新信息。\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", [... omitted end of long features. Alpamayo 1 is designed specifically for trajectory prediction with Chain-of-Causation reasoning, producing trajectory + reasoning trace outputs.\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":119,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"No. The current 10B model release has **not** undergone RL post-training. While the paper describes RL stages for improving reasoning quality and action consistency, this release focuses on the supervised learning components. See the [License](#license) section and the [HuggingFace Model Card](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) for details.\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":140,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"alpamayo/\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":144,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"│   └── alpamayo_r1/\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":183,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"- **Model weights**: Non-commercial license - see [HuggingFace Model Card](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) for details.\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":187,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"Alpamayo 1 is a pre-trained reasoning model designed to accelerate research and development in the autonomous vehicle (AV) domain. It is intended to serve as a foundation for a range of AV-related use cases-from instantiating an end-to-end backbone for autonomous driving to enabling reasoning-based auto-labeling tools. In short, it should be viewed as a building block for developing customized AV applications.\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":191,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"- Alpamayo 1 is provided solely for research, experimentation, and evaluation purposes.\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":192,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"- Alpamayo 1 is not a fully fledged driving stack. Among other limitations, it lacks access to critical real-world sensor inputs, does not incorporate required diverse and redundant safety mechanisms, and has not undergone automotive-grade validation for deployment.\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":198,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"If you use Alpamayo 1 in your research, please cite:\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":201,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"@article{nvidia2025alpamayo,\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":202,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"      title={{Alpamayo-R1}: Bridging Reasoning and Action Prediction for Generalizable Autonomous Driving in the Long Tail},\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":205,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"      journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2511.00088},\\\\\\\"}]},{\\\\\\\"file\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"./CONTRIBUTING.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"matches\\\\\\\":[{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":1,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"## Alpamayo 1 OSS Contribution Rules\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":5,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"- All enhancement, bugfix, or change requests must begin with the creation of an [Alpamayo 1 Issue Request](https://github.com/NVlabs/alpamayo/issues).\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":6,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"  - The issue request must be reviewed by Alpamayo 1 researchers and approved prior to code review.\\\\\\\"},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":12,\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"- To maintain consistency in code formatting and style, you should also run &#96;pre-commit format&#96; on the modified sources with the provided configuration file. [],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_c397b75a-9299-4f55-9247-3594700b77d\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_c397b75a-9299-4f55-9247-3594700b77d\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/README.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/README.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/README.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"&lt;div align=\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"center\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n# 🏔️ Alpamayo 1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Bridging Reasoning and Action Prediction for Generalizable Autonomous Driving\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n[![HuggingFace](https://img.shields.io/badge/🤗%20Model-Alpamayo--R1--10B-blue)](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B)\\\\\\\\n[![arXiv](https://img.shields.io/badge/arXiv-2511.00088-b31b1b.svg)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.00088)\\\\\\\\n[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-green.svg)](./LICENSE)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;/div&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n_Note: Following the release of [NVIDIA Alpamayo](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/alpamayo-autonomous-vehicle-development) at CES 2026, Alpamayo-R1 has been renamed to Alpamayo 1._\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&gt; **📖 Please read the [HuggingFace Model Card](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) first!**\\\\\\\\n&gt; The model card contains comprehensive details on model architecture, inputs/outputs, licensing, and tested hardware configurations. This GitHub README focuses on setup, usage, and frequently asked questions.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Requirements\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Requirement | Specification |\\\\\\\\n|-------------|---------------|\\\\\\\\n| **Python** | 3.12.x (see &#96;pyproject.toml&#96;) |\\\\\\\\n| **GPU** | NVIDIA GPU with ≥24 GB VRAM (e.g., RTX 3090, RTX 4090, A5000, H100) |\\\\\\\\n| **OS** | Linux (tested); other platforms unverified |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&gt; ⚠️ **Note**: GPUs with less than 24 GB VRAM will likely encounter CUDA out-of-memory errors.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Installation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 1. Install uv (if not already installed)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\ncurl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh\\\\\\\\nexport PATH=\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 2. Set up the environment\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\nuv venv ar1_venv\\\\\\\\nsource ar1_venv/bin/activate\\\\\\\\nuv sync --active\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 3. Authenticate with HuggingFace\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe model requires access to gated resources. Request access here:\\\\\\\\n- 🤗 [Physical AI AV Dataset](https://huggingface.co/datasets/nvidia/PhysicalAI-Autonomous-Vehicles)\\\\\\\\n- 🤗 [Alpamayo Model Weights](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThen authenticate using the HuggingFace CLI:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n# Install huggingface-cli if not already installed (included in transformers)\\\\\\\\npip install huggingface_hub\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n# Login with your token\\\\\\\\nhuggingface-cli login\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nGet your access token at: https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&gt; 💡 **Tip**: For more details on HuggingFace authentication, see the [official documentation](https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/guides/cli).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Running Inference\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Test script\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNOTE: This script will download both some example data (relatively small) and the model weights (22 GB).\\\\\\\\nThe latter can be particularly slow depending on network bandwidth.\\\\\\\\nFor reference, it takes around 2.5 minutes on a 100 MB/s wired connection.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\npython src/alpamayo_r1/test_inference.py\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIn case you would like to obtain more trajectories and reasoning traces, please feel free to change\\\\\\\\nthe &#96;num_traj_samples=1&#96; argument to a higher number (Line 60).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Interactive notebook\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWe provide a notebook with similar inference code at &#96;notebook/inference.ipynb&#96;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Relationship with the Paper\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAlpamayo 1 implements the architecture described in our paper [*\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Alpamayo-R1: Bridging Reasoning and Action Prediction for Generalizable Autonomous Driving in the Long Tail\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"*](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.00088), including:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Feature | Paper Description | This Release (v1.0) |\\\\\\\\n|---------|-------------------|---------------------|\\\\\\\\n| **Chain-of-Causation (CoC) reasoning** | Hybrid auto-labeling with human in the loop for reasoning traces | ✅ Included |\\\\\\\\n| **Vision-Language-Action architecture** | Cosmos-Reason backbone + action expert | ✅ Included |\\\\\\\\n| **Trajectory prediction** | 6.4s horizon, 64 waypoints at 10 Hz | ✅ Included |\\\\\\\\n| **RL post-training** | Reinforcement learning for reasoning/action consistency | ❌ Not in this release |\\\\\\\\n| **Route/navigation conditioning** | Explicit navigation or route inputs | ❌ Not in this release |\\\\\\\\n| **Meta-actions/General VQA** | High-level behavior and visual question answering | ❌ Not in this release |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe current release focuses on the core supervised learning components. RL post-training and route conditioning are potential candidates for future releases. Stay tuned!\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;details&gt;\\\\\\\\n&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Does the 10B model accept navigation/route inputs?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhile we have experimented with route conditioning capabilities, the released model does **not** include this feature. The current release takes multi-camera video and egomotion history as inputs, without explicit navigation or route inputs (e.g., waypoints, turn-by-turn navigation instructions).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;/details&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;details&gt;\\\\\\\\n&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Does the model produce meta-actions or support general VQA?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhile we have experimented with meta-action and general VQA capabilities, the released model does **not** include these features. Alpamayo 1 is designed specifically for trajectory prediction with Chain-of-Causation reasoning, producing trajectory + reasoning trace outputs.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;/details&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;details&gt;\\\\\\\\n&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Was the 10B model post-trained with Reinforcement Learning (RL)?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNo. The current 10B model release has **not** undergone RL post-training. While the paper describes RL stages for improving reasoning quality and action consistency, this release focuses on the supervised learning components. As mentioned above, we may release RL post-trained models in future releases.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;/details&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;details&gt;\\\\\\\\n&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;What are the minimum GPU requirements?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nYou need an NVIDIA GPU with at least **24 GB VRAM** for inference. Tested configurations include RTX 3090, A100, and H100. Running on GPUs with less memory (e.g., 16 GB) will likely result in CUDA out-of-memory errors.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;/details&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;details&gt;\\\\\\\\n&lt;summary&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Can I use this model in production / commercial applications?&lt;/strong&gt;&lt;/summary&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNo. The model weights are released under a **non-commercial license**. This release is intended for research, experimentation, and evaluation purposes only. See the [License](#license) section and the [HuggingFace Model Card](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) for details.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&lt;/details&gt;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Project Structure\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\nalpamayo/\\\\\\\\n├── notebook/\\\\\\\\n│   └── inference.ipynb                  # Example notebook\\\\\\\\n├── src/\\\\\\\\n│   └── alpamayo_r1/\\\\\\\\n│       ├── action_space/\\\\\\\\n│       │   └── ...                      # Action space definitions\\\\\\\\n│       ├── diffusion/\\\\\\\\n│       │   └── ...                      # Diffusion model components\\\\\\\\n│       ├── geometry/\\\\\\\\n│       │   └── ...                      # Geometry utilities and modules\\\\\\\\n│       ├── models/\\\\\\\\n│       │   ├── ...                      # Model components and utils functions\\\\\\\\n│       ├── __init__.py                  # Package marker\\\\\\\\n│       ├── config.py                    # Model and experiment configuration\\\\\\\\n│       ├── helper.py                    # Utility functions\\\\\\\\n│       ├── load_physical_aiavdataset.py # Dataset loader\\\\\\\\n│       ├── test_inference.py            # Inference test script\\\\\\\\n├── pyproject.toml                       # Project dependencies\\\\\\\\n└── uv.lock                              # Locked dependency versions\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Troubleshooting\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Flash Attention issues\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe model uses Flash Attention 2 by default. If you encounter compatibility issues:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\\\\\\\n# Use PyTorch's scaled dot-product attention instead\\\\\\\\nconfig.attn_implementation = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sdpa\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### CUDA out-of-memory errors\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you encounter OOM errors:\\\\\\\\n1. Ensure you have a GPU with at least 24 GB VRAM\\\\\\\\n2. Reduce &#96;num_traj_samples&#96; if generating multiple trajectories\\\\\\\\n3. Close other GPU-intensive applications\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## License\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Inference code**: Apache License 2.0 - see [LICENSE](./LICENSE) for details.\\\\\\\\n- **Model weights**: Non-commercial license - see [HuggingFace Model Card](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) for details.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Disclaimer\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAlpamayo 1 is a pre-trained reasoning model designed to accelerate research and development in the autonomous vehicle (AV) domain. It is intended to serve as a foundation for a range of AV-related use cases-from instantiating an end-to-end backbone for autonomous driving to enabling reasoning-based auto-labeling tools. In short, it should be viewed as a building block for developing customized AV applications.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nImportant notes:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Alpamayo 1 is provided solely for research, experimentation, and evaluation purposes.\\\\\\\\n- Alpamayo 1 is not a fully fledged driving stack. Among other limitations, it lacks access to critical real-world sensor inputs, does not incorporate required diverse and redundant safety mechanisms, and has not undergone automotive-grade validation for deployment.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nBy using this model, you acknowledge that it is a research tool intended to support scientific inquiry, benchmarking, and exploration—not a substitute for a certified AV stack. The developers and contributors disclaim any responsibility or liability for the use of the model or its outputs.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Citation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nIf you use Alpamayo 1 in your research, please cite:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bibtex\\\\\\\\n@article{nvidia2025alpamayo,\\\\\\\\n      title={{Alpamayo-R1}: Bridging Reasoning and Action Prediction for Generalizable Autonomous rkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_0a064ffd-803b-4f5c-9704-8127b566a2c\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_0a064ffd-803b-4f5c-9704-8127b566a2c\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/src/alpamayo_r1/load_physical_aiavdataset.py\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/src/alpamayo_r1/load_physical_aiavdataset.py\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/src/alpamayo_r1/load_physical_aiavdataset.py\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# All rights reserved.\\\\\\\\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\\\\\\\n#\\\\\\\\n# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"License\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\\\\\\\n# You may obtain a copy of the License at\\\\\\\\n#\\\\\\\\n# http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\\\\\\\n#\\\\\\\\n# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\\\\\\\n# distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"AS IS\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" BASIS,\\\\\\\\n# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\\\\\\\n# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\\\\\\\n# limitations under the License.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Load data from physical_ai_av.PhysicalAIAVDatasetInterface for model inference.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nfrom typing import Any\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport numpy as np\\\\\\\\nimport physical_ai_av\\\\\\\\nimport scipy.spatial.transform as spt\\\\\\\\nimport torch\\\\\\\\nfrom einops import rearrange\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ndef load_physical_aiavdataset(\\\\\\\\n    clip_id: str,\\\\\\\\n    t0_us: int = 5_100_000,\\\\\\\\n    avdi: physical_ai_av.PhysicalAIAVDatasetInterface | None = None,\\\\\\\\n    maybe_stream: bool = True,\\\\\\\\n    num_history_steps: int = 16,\\\\\\\\n    num_future_steps: int = 64,\\\\\\\\n    time_step: float = 0.1,\\\\\\\\n    camera_features: list | None = None,\\\\\\\\n    num_frames: int = 4,\\\\\\\\n) -&gt; dict[str, Any]:\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Load data from physical_ai_av for model inference.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    This function loads a sample from the physical_ai_av dataset and converts it\\\\\\\\n    to the format expected by AlpamayoR1 model inference.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Args:\\\\\\\\n        clip_id: The clip ID to load data from. Can be obtained from vla_golden.parquet.\\\\\\\\n        t0_us: The timestamp (in microseconds) at which to sample the trajectory.\\\\\\\\n            If None, uses a timestamp 5.1s seconds into the clip.\\\\\\\\n        avdi: Optional pre-initialized PhysicalAIAVDatasetInterface. If None, creates one.\\\\\\\\n        maybe_stream: Whether to stream data from HuggingFace (if not downloaded locally).\\\\\\\\n        num_history_steps: Number of history trajectory steps (default: 16 for 1.6s at 10Hz).\\\\\\\\n        num_future_steps: Number of future trajectory steps (default: 64 for 6.4s at 10Hz).\\\\\\\\n        time_step: Time step between trajectory points in seconds (default: 0.1s = 10Hz).\\\\\\\\n        camera_features: List of camera features to load. If None, uses 4 cameras:\\\\\\\\n            [CAMERA_FRONT_WIDE_120FOV, CAMERA_FRONT_TELE_30FOV,\\\\\\\\n             CAMERA_CROSS_LEFT_120FOV, CAMERA_CROSS_RIGHT_120FOV].\\\\\\\\n        num_frames: Number of frames per camera to load (default: 4).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Returns:\\\\\\\\n        A dictionary with the following keys:\\\\\\\\n            - image_frames: torch.Tensor of shape (N_cameras, num_frames, 3, H, W)\\\\\\\\n            - camera_indices: torch.Tensor of shape (N_cameras,)\\\\\\\\n            - ego_history_xyz: torch.Tensor of shape (1, 1, num_history_steps, 3)\\\\\\\\n            - ego_history_rot: torch.Tensor of shape (1, 1, num_history_steps, 3, 3)\\\\\\\\n            - ego_future_xyz: torch.Tensor of shape (1, 1, num_future_steps, 3)\\\\\\\\n            - ego_future_rot: torch.Tensor of shape (1, 1, num_future_steps, 3, 3)\\\\\\\\n            - relative_timestamps: torch.Tensor of shape (N_cameras, num_frames)\\\\\\\\n            - absolute_timestamps: torch.Tensor of shape (N_cameras, num_frames)\\\\\\\\n            - t0_us: The t0 timestamp used\\\\\\\\n            - clip_id: The clip ID\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    if avdi is None:\\\\\\\\n        avdi = physical_ai_av.PhysicalAIAVDatasetInterface()\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    if camera_features is None:\\\\\\\\n        camera_features = [\\\\\\\\n            avdi.features.CAMERA.CAMERA_CROSS_LEFT_120FOV,\\\\\\\\n            avdi.features.CAMERA.CAMERA_FRONT_WIDE_120FOV,\\\\\\\\n            avdi.features.CAMERA.CAMERA_CROSS_RIGHT_120FOV,\\\\\\\\n            avdi.features.CAMERA.CAMERA_FRONT_TELE_30FOV,\\\\\\\\n        ]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    camera_name_to_index = {\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"camera_cross_left_120fov\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 0,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"camera_front_wide_120fov\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 1,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"camera_cross_right_120fov\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 2,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"camera_rear_left_70fov\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 3,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"camera_rear_tele_30fov\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 4,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"camera_rear_right_70fov\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 5,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"camera_front_tele_30fov\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 6,\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Load egomotion data\\\\\\\\n    egomotion = avdi.get_clip_feature(\\\\\\\\n        clip_id,\\\\\\\\n        avdi.features.LABELS.EGOMOTION,\\\\\\\\n        maybe_stream=maybe_stream,\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    assert t0_us &gt; num_history_steps * time_step * 1_000_000, (\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"t0_us must be greater than the history time range\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Compute timestamps for trajectory sampling\\\\\\\\n    # History: [..., t0-0.2s, t0-0.1s, t0] (num_history_steps points ending at t0)\\\\\\\\n    # Future: [t0+0.1s, t0+0.2s, ..., t0+6.4s] (num_future_steps points after t0)\\\\\\\\n    history_offsets_us = np.arange(\\\\\\\\n        -(num_history_steps - 1) * time_step * 1_000_000,\\\\\\\\n        time_step * 1_000_000 / 2,\\\\\\\\n        time_step * 1_000_000,\\\\\\\\n    ).astype(np.int64)\\\\\\\\n    history_timestamps = t0_us + history_offsets_us\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    future_offsets_us = np.arange(\\\\\\\\n        time_step * 1_000_000,\\\\\\\\n        (num_future_steps + 0.5) * time_step * 1_000_000,\\\\\\\\n        time_step * 1_000_000,\\\\\\\\n    ).astype(np.int64)\\\\\\\\n    future_timestamps = t0_us + future_offsets_us\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Get egomotion at history and future timestamps\\\\\\\\n    ego_history = egomotion(history_timestamps)\\\\\\\\n    ego_history_xyz = ego_history.pose.translation  # (num_history_steps, 3)\\\\\\\\n    ego_history_quat = ego_history.pose.rotation.as_quat()  # (num_history_steps, 4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    ego_future = egomotion(future_timestamps)\\\\\\\\n    ego_future_xyz = ego_future.pose.translation  # (num_future_steps, 3)\\\\\\\\n    ego_future_quat = ego_future.pose.rotation.as_quat()  # (num_future_steps, 4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Transform to local frame (relative to t0 pose)\\\\\\\\n    # The model expects trajectories in the ego frame at t0.\\\\\\\\n    # Transformation: xyz_local = R_t0^{-1} @ (xyz_world - xyz_t0)\\\\\\\\n    t0_xyz = ego_history_xyz[-1].copy()  # Position at t0\\\\\\\\n    t0_quat = ego_history_quat[-1].copy()  # Orientation at t0\\\\\\\\n    t0_rot = spt.Rotation.from_quat(t0_quat)\\\\\\\\n    t0_rot_inv = t0_rot.inv()\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Transform history positions to local frame\\\\\\\\n    ego_history_xyz_local = t0_rot_inv.apply(ego_history_xyz - t0_xyz)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Transform future positions to local frame\\\\\\\\n    ego_future_xyz_local = t0_rot_inv.apply(ego_future_xyz - t0_xyz)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Transform rotations to local frame\\\\\\\\n    ego_history_rot_local = (t0_rot_inv * spt.Rotation.from_quat(ego_history_quat)).as_matrix()\\\\\\\\n    ego_future_rot_local = (t0_rot_inv * spt.Rotation.from_quat(ego_future_quat)).as_matrix()\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Convert to torch tensors with batch dimensions: (B=1, n_traj_group=1, T, ...)\\\\\\\\n    ego_history_xyz_tensor = (\\\\\\\\n        torch.from_numpy(ego_history_xyz_local).float().unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n    ego_history_rot_tensor = (\\\\\\\\n        torch.from_numpy(ego_history_rot_local).float().unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n    ego_future_xyz_tensor = torch.from_numpy(ego_future_xyz_local).float().unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)\\\\\\\\n    ego_future_rot_tensor = torch.from_numpy(ego_future_rot_local).float().unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Load camera images\\\\\\\\n    image_frames_list = []\\\\\\\\n    camera_indices_list = []\\\\\\\\n    timestamps_list = []\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Image timestamps: if num_frames=4, load at [t0-0.3s, t0-0.2s, t0-0.1s, t0]\\\\\\\\n    image_timestamps = np.array(\\\\\\\\n        [t0_us - (num_frames - 1 - i) * int(time_step * 1_000_000) for i in range(num_frames)],\\\\\\\\n        dtype=np.int64,\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    for cam_feature in camera_features:\\\\\\\\n        camera = avdi.get_clip_feature(\\\\\\\\n            clip_id,\\\\\\\\n            cam_feature,\\\\\\\\n            maybe_stream=maybe_stream,\\\\\\\\n        )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # frames: (num_frames, H, W, 3) uint8\\\\\\\\n        frames, frame_timestamps = camera.decode_images_from_timestamps(image_timestamps)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Convert to (num_frames, 3, H, W) for model input\\\\\\\\n        frames_tensor = torch.from_numpy(frames)\\\\\\\\n        frames_tensor = rearrange(frames_tensor, \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"t h w c -&gt; t c h w\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Extract camera name from feature path\\\\\\\\n        if isinstance(cam_feature, str):\\\\\\\\n            cam_name = cam_feature.split(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")[-1] if \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" in cam_feature else cam_feature\\\\\\\\n            cam_name = cam_name.lower()\\\\\\\\n        else:\\\\\\\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Unexpected camera feature type: {type(cam_feature)}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n        cam_idx = camera_name_to_index.get(cam_name, 0)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        image_frames_list.append(frames_tensor)\\\\\\\\n        camera_indices_list.append(cam_idx)\\\\\\\\n        timestamps_list.append(torch.from_numpy(frame_timestamps.astype(np.int64)))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Stack and sort by camera index for consistent ordering\\\\\\\\n    image_frames = torch.stack(image_frames_list, dim=0)  # (N_cameras, num_frames, 3, H, W)\\\\\\\\n    camera_indices = torch.tensor(camera_indices_list, dtype=torch.int64)  # (N_cameras,)\\\\\\\\n    all_timestamps = torch.stack(timestamps_list, dim=0)  # (N_cameras, num_frames)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Sort by camera index to ensure consistent ordering [0, 1, 2, 6] instead of arbitrary order\\\\\\\\n    sort_order = torch.argsort(camera_indices)\\\\\\\\n    image_frames = image_frames[sort_order]\\\\\\\\n    camera_indices = camera_indices[sort_order]\\\\\\\\n    all_timestamps = all_timestamps[sort_order]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Compute relative timestamps in seconds\\\\\\\\n    camera_tmin = all_timestamps.min()\\\\\\\\n    relative_timestamps = (all_timestamps - camera_tmin).float() * 1e-6  # (N_cameras, num_frames)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    return {\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"image_frames\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": image_frames,  # (N_cameras, num_frames, 3, H, W)\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"camera_indices\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": camera_indices,  # (N_cameras,)\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ego_history_xyz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": ego_history_xyz_tensor,  # (1, 1, num_history_steps, 3)\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ego_history_rot\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": ego_history_rot_tensor,  # (1, 1, num_history_steps, 3, 3)\\\\\\\\n num_future_steps, 3, 3)\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"relative_timestamps\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": relative_timestamps,  # (N_cameras, num_frames)\\\\\\\\n import Literal\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport torch\\\\\\\\nfrom alpamayo_r1.diffusion.base import BaseDiffusion, StepFn\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nclass FlowMatching(BaseDiffusion):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Flow Matching model.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    References:\\\\\\\\n    Flow Matching for Generative Modeling\\\\\\\\n        https://arxiv.org/pdf/2210.02747\\\\\\\\n    Guided Flows for Generative Modeling and Decision Making\\\\\\\\n        https://arxiv.org/pdf/2311.13443\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\n        int_method: Literal[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"euler\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"euler\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        num_inference_steps: int = 5,\\\\\\\\n        *args,\\\\\\\\n        **kwargs,\\\\\\\\n    ):\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Initialize the FlowMatching model.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            int_method: The integration method used in inference.\\\\\\\\n            num_inference_steps: The number of inference steps.\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)\\\\\\\\n        self.int_method = int_method\\\\\\\\n        self.num_inference_steps = num_inference_steps\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    @torch.no_grad()\\\\\\\\n    def sample(\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\n        batch_size: int,\\\\\\\\n        step_fn: StepFn,\\\\\\\\n        device: torch.device = torch.device(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"cpu\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"),\\\\\\\\n        return_all_steps: bool = False,\\\\\\\\n        inference_step: int | None = None,\\\\\\\\n        int_method: Literal[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"euler\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] | None = None,\\\\\\\\n        *args,\\\\\\\\n        **kwargs,\\\\\\\\n    ) -&gt; torch.Tensor | tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Sample data from the model.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            batch_size: The batch size.\\\\\\\\n            step_fn: The denoising step function.\\\\\\\\n            device: The device to use.\\\\\\\\n            return_all_steps: Whether to return all steps.\\\\\\\\n            inference_step: The number of inference steps. (override self.num_inference_steps)\\\\\\\\n            int_method: The integration method used in inference. (override self.int_method)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Returns:\\\\\\\\n            torch.Tensor | tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\\\\\\\\n                The final sampled tensor [B, *x_dims] if return_all_steps is False,\\\\\\\\n                otherwise a tuple of all sampled tensors [B, T, *x_dims] and the time steps [T].\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        int_method = int_method or self.int_method\\\\\\\\n        inference_step = inference_step or self.num_inference_steps\\\\\\\\n        if int_method == \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"euler\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\n            return self._euler(\\\\\\\\n                batch_size=batch_size,\\\\\\\\n                step_fn=step_fn,\\\\\\\\n                device=device,\\\\\\\\n                return_all_steps=return_all_steps,\\\\\\\\n                inference_step=inference_step,\\\\\\\\n            )\\\\\\\\n        else:\\\\\\\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Invalid integration method: {int_method}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def _euler(\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\n        batch_size: int,\\\\\\\\n        step_fn: StepFn,\\\\\\\\n        device: torch.device = torch.device(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"cpu\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"),\\\\\\\\n        return_all_steps: bool = False,\\\\\\\\n        inference_step: int | None = None,\\\\\\\\n    ) -&gt; torch.Tensor | tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Euler integration for flow matching.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            batch_size: The batch size.\\\\\\\\n            step_fn: The denoising step function.\\\\\\\\n            device: The device to use.\\\\\\\\n            return_all_steps: Whether to return all steps.\\\\\\\\n            inference_step: The inference step.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Returns:\\\\\\\\n            torch.Tensor | tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\\\\\\\\n                The final sampled tensor [B, *x_dims] if return_all_steps is False,\\\\\\\\n                otherwise a tuple of all sampled tensors [B, T, *x_dims] and the time steps [T].\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        x = torch.randn(batch_size, *self.x_dims, device=device)\\\\\\\\n        time_steps = torch.linspace(0.0, 1.0, inference_step + 1, device=device)\\\\\\\\n        n_dim = len(self.x_dims)\\\\\\\\n        if return_all_steps:\\\\\\\\n            all_steps = [x]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        for i in range(inference_step):\\\\\\\\n            dt = time_steps[i + 1] - time_steps[i]\\\\\\\\n            dt = dt.view(1, *[1] * n_dim).expand(batch_size, *[1] * n_dim)\\\\\\\\n            t_start = time_steps[i].view(1, *[1] * n_dim).expand(batch_size, *[1] * n_dim)\\\\\\\\n            v = step_fn(x=x, t=t_start)\\\\\\\\n            x = x + dt * v\\\\\\\\n            if return_all_steps:\\\\\\\\n                all_steps.append(x)\\\\\\\\n        if return_all_steps:\\\\\\\\n            return torch.stack(all_steps, dim=1), time_steps\\\\\\\\n        return math\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport torch\\\\\\\\nfrom torch import nn\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nclass RMSNorm(torch.nn.Module):\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(self, dim: int, eps: float):\\\\\\\\n        super().__init__()\\\\\\\\n        self.eps = eps\\\\\\\\n        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(dim))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def _norm(self, x):\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Normalize the input tensor.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        return x * torch.rsqrt(x.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + self.eps)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def forward(self, x):\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Normalize the input tensor.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        output = self._norm(x.float()).type_as(x)\\\\\\\\n        return output * self.weight\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nclass MLPEncoder(nn.Module):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Basic MLP encoder.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(self, num_input_feats: int, num_enc_layers: int, hidden_size: int, outdim: int):\\\\\\\\n        super().__init__()\\\\\\\\n        assert 1 &lt;= num_enc_layers, f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{num_enc_layers=} must be &gt;= 1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        enc_layers = [\\\\\\\\n            nn.Linear(num_input_feats, hidden_size),\\\\\\\\n            nn.SiLU(),\\\\\\\\n        ]\\\\\\\\n        for layeri in range(num_enc_layers):\\\\\\\\n            if layeri &lt; num_enc_layers - 1:\\\\\\\\n                enc_layers.extend(\\\\\\\\n                    [\\\\\\\\n                        RMSNorm(hidden_size, eps=1e-5),\\\\\\\\n                        nn.Linear(hidden_size, hidden_size),\\\\\\\\n                        nn.SiLU(),\\\\\\\\n                    ]\\\\\\\\n                )\\\\\\\\n            else:\\\\\\\\n                enc_layers.extend(\\\\\\\\n                    [\\\\\\\\n                        RMSNorm(hidden_size, eps=1e-5),\\\\\\\\n                        nn.Linear(hidden_size, outdim),\\\\\\\\n                    ]\\\\\\\\n                )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        self.trunk = nn.Sequential(*enc_layers)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -&gt; torch.Tensor:\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"(B, C) -&gt; (B, outdim)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        return self.trunk(x)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nclass FourierEncoderV2(nn.Module):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Improved Fourier feature encoder with logarithmically-spaced frequencies.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(self, dim: int, max_freq: float = 100.0):\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Initialize the Fourier encoder V2.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            dim: Output dimension of the encoder. Must be even as it's split into\\\\\\\\n                sine and cosine components.\\\\\\\\n            max_freq: Maximum frequency for the logarithmic frequency spacing.\\\\\\\\n                Defaults to 100.0.\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        super().__init__()\\\\\\\\n        half = dim // 2\\\\\\\\n        freqs = torch.logspace(0, math.log10(max_freq), steps=half)\\\\\\\\n        self.out_dim = dim\\\\\\\\n        self.register_buffer(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"freqs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", freqs[None, :])  # (1, half)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -&gt; torch.Tensor:\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Forward pass of the Fourier encoder V2.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            x: Input tensor of arbitrary shape (..., ).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Returns:\\\\\\\\n            Fourier-encoded features of shape (..., dim).\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        arg = x[..., None] * self.freqs * 2 * torch.pi  # (*, half_dim)\\\\\\\\n        return torch.cat([torch.sin(arg), torch.cos(arg)], -1) * math.sqrt(2)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nclass PerWaypointActionInProjV2(torch.nn.Module):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Improved per-waypoint action input projection module.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    It uses FourierEncoderV2 with logarithmically-spaced frequencies and includes layer normalization. Projects\\\\\\\\n    action sequences with timestep information into a higher-dimensional representation.\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\n        in_dims: list[int],\\\\\\\\n        out_dim: int,\\\\\\\\n        num_enc_layers: int = 4,\\\\\\\\n        hidden_size: int = 1024,\\\\\\\\n        max_freq: float = 100.0,\\\\\\\\n        num_fourier_feats: int = 20,\\\\\\\\n    ):\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Initialize the per-waypoint action projection module V2.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            in_dims: List of input dimensions. The last element specifies the number\\\\\\\\n                of action dimensions to encode separately.\\\\\\\\n            out_dim: Output dimension of the projection.\\\\\\\\n            num_enc_layers: Number of layers in the MLP encoder. Defaults to 4.\\\\\\\\n            hidden_size: Hidden dimension size of the MLP encoder. Defaults to 1024.\\\\\\\\n            max_freq: Maximum frequency for the Fourier encoding. Defaults to 100.0.\\\\\\\\n            num_fourier_feats: Number of Fourier features for encoding. Defaults to 20.\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        super().__init__()\\\\\\\\n        self.in_dims = in_dims\\\\\\\\n        self.out_dim = out_dim\\\\\\\\n        sinus = []\\\\\\\\n        for _ in range(in_dims[-1]):\\\\\\\\n            sinus.append(FourierEncoderV2(dim=num_fourier_feats, max_freq=max_freq))\\\\\\\\n        self.sinus = nn.ModuleList(sinus)\\\\\\\\n        self.timestep_fourier_encoder = FourierEncoderV2(dim=num_fourier_feats, max_freq=max_freq)\\\\\\\\n        num_input_feats = sum(s.out_dim for s in self.sinus) + self.timestep_fourier_encoder.out_dim\\\\\\\\n        self.encoder = MLPEncoder(\\\\\\\\n            num_input_feats=num_input_feats,\\\\\\\\n            num_enc_layers=num_enc_layers,\\\\\\\\n            hidden_size=hidden_size,\\\\\\\\n            outdim=out_dim,\\\\\\\\n        )\\\\\\\\n        self.norm = nn.LayerNorm(out_dim)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, timesteps: torch.Tensor) -&gt; torch.Tensor:\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Forward pass of the per-waypoint action projection V2.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            x: Action tensor of shape (batch_size, num_waypoints, action_dim).\\\\\\\\n            timesteps: Timestep tensor of shape (batch_size, ...). The last dimension\\\\\\\\n                is used for encoding.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Returns:\\\\\\\\n            Normalized projected action features of shape\\\\\\\\n            (batch_size, num_waypoints, out_dim).\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        B, T, _ = x.shape\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        action_feats = torch.cat([s(x[:, :, i]) for i, s in enumerate(self.sinus)], dim=-1)\\\\\\\\n        timestep_feats = self.timestep_fourier_encoder(timesteps[..., -1])\\\\\\\\n        timestep_feats = timestep_feats.repeat(1, T, 1)\\\\\\\\n        x = torch.cat((action_feats, timestep_feats), dim=-1)\\\\\\\\n        return self.norm(self.encoder(x.flatten(0, 1)).reshape(B, T, -1))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":167}\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"6f037079-0806-483a-8765-780646b713f8\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"628e6b83-19b9-441c-908b-0558f6079ca6\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-04T19:01:04.342Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\ helper.create_message(data[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"image_frames\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"].flatten(0, 1))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nmodel = AlpamayoR1.from_pretrained(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", dtype=torch.bfloat16).to(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"cuda\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\nprocessor = helper.get_processor(model.tokenizer)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ninputs = processor.apply_chat_template(\\\\\\\\n data[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ego_history_rot\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"],\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nmodel_inputs = helper.to_device(model_inputs, \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"cuda\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\nif torch.cuda.is_available():\\\\\\\\n    torch.cuda.synchronize()\\\\\\\\nt_end_load = time.perf_counter()\\\\\\\\ntiming_load_ms = (t_end_load - t_start_load) * 1000.0\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ntorch.cuda.manual_seed_all(42)\\\\\\\\nwith torch.autocast(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"cuda\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\\\\\\\\n    pred_xyz, pred_rot, extra = model.sample_trajectories_from_data_with_vlm_rollout(\\\\\\\\n        data=model_inputs,\\\\\\\\n        top_p=0.98,\\\\\\\\n        temperature=0.6,\\\\\\\\n        num_traj_samples=1,  # Feel free to raise this for more output trajectories and CoC traces.\\\\\\\\n        max_generation_length=256,\\\\\\\\n        return_extra=True,\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\nif torch.cuda.is_available():\\\\\\\\n extra[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"cot\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"][0])\\\\\\\\nprint(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n=== 耗时统计 ===\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\nprint(f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"数据加载与预处理: {timing_load_ms:.2f} ms\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\nprint(f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"轨迹生成:         {timing_traj_gen_ms:.2f} ms\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\nprint(f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"总耗时:           {timing_total_ms:.2f} ms\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ngt_xy = data[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ego_future_xyz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"].cpu()[0, 0, :, :2].T.numpy()\\\\\\\\npred_xy = pred_xyz.cpu().numpy()[0, 0, :, :, :2].transpose(0, 2, logging\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport torch\\\\\\\\nfrom transformers import AutoTokenizer, StoppingCriteria\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ndef to_special_token(token: str) -&gt; str:\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Convert a token to a special token.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    return \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"&lt;|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" + token + \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"|&gt;\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ndef extract_traj_tokens(\\\\\\\\n    output_tokens: torch.Tensor,\\\\\\\\n    special_token_ids: dict[str, int],\\\\\\\\n    tokens_per_future_traj: int,\\\\\\\\n    future_token_start_idx: int,\\\\\\\\n    traj_tokenizer_vocab_size: int,\\\\\\\\n) -&gt; torch.Tensor:\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Extract the trajectory tokens from the output tokens (parallel/vectorized version).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    This is a fully vectorized implementation that processes all batches in parallel\\\\\\\\n    without looping over the batch dimension.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    The output tokens to be [...&lt;|cot_end|&gt;&lt;|meta_action_start|&gt;...&lt;meta_action_end&gt;\\\\\\\\n        &lt;|future_traj_start|&gt;]&lt;|future_traj|&gt;...&lt;|future_traj_end|&gt;.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Args:\\\\\\\\n        output_tokens (torch.Tensor): The output tokens of shape [B, L].\\\\\\\\n        special_token_ids (dict[str, int]): Dictionary mapping special token names to their IDs.\\\\\\\\n        tokens_per_future_traj (int): Expected number of trajectory tokens.\\\\\\\\n        future_token_start_idx (int): The starting index for trajectory tokens in vocabulary.\\\\\\\\n        traj_tokenizer_vocab_size (int): Size of the trajectory tokenizer vocabulary.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Returns:\\\\\\\\n        torch.Tensor: The trajectory tokens of shape [B, tokens_per_future_traj].\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    batch_size, seq_len = output_tokens.shape\\\\\\\\n    device = output_tokens.device\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Initialize output tensor\\\\\\\\n    traj_tokens = torch.zeros(\\\\\\\\n        (batch_size, tokens_per_future_traj), dtype=output_tokens.dtype, device=device\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # For each batch, find the first occurrence of end token\\\\\\\\n    # If no end token, use seq_len as the end position\\\\\\\\n    end_mask = output_tokens == special_token_ids[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"traj_future_end\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]\\\\\\\\n    end_positions = torch.where(\\\\\\\\n        end_mask.any(dim=1),\\\\\\\\n        end_mask.int().argmax(dim=1),\\\\\\\\n        torch.full((batch_size,), seq_len, dtype=torch.long, device=device),\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # For each batch, find the last occurrence of start token\\\\\\\\n    # We reverse the sequence to find the last occurrence\\\\\\\\n    start_mask = output_tokens == special_token_ids[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"traj_future_start\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]\\\\\\\\n    start_mask_reversed = torch.flip(start_mask, dims=[1])\\\\\\\\n    last_start_positions_reversed = start_mask_reversed.int().argmax(dim=1)\\\\\\\\n    start_positions = seq_len - 1 - last_start_positions_reversed\\\\\\\\n    start_positions = torch.where(\\\\\\\\n        start_mask.any(dim=1),\\\\\\\\n        start_positions,\\\\\\\\n        torch.full((batch_size,), -1, dtype=torch.long, device=device),\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Create a range tensor for indexing [B, seq_len]\\\\\\\\n    range_tensor = torch.arange(seq_len, device=device).unsqueeze(0).expand(batch_size, -1)\\\\\\\\n    valid_mask = (range_tensor &gt; start_positions.unsqueeze(1)) &amp; (\\\\\\\\n        range_tensor &lt; end_positions.unsqueeze(1)\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n    extracted_tokens = torch.where(valid_mask, output_tokens, torch.zeros_like(output_tokens))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Check for mismatches in token count\\\\\\\\n    n_valid_tokens = valid_mask.sum(dim=1)\\\\\\\\n    mismatch_mask = n_valid_tokens != tokens_per_future_traj\\\\\\\\n    if mismatch_mask.any():\\\\\\\\n        for idx in mismatch_mask.nonzero(as_tuple=True)[0]:\\\\\\\\n            logger.warning(\\\\\\\\n                f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Batch {idx}: Number of tokens is not equal to the expected number. \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n                f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Expected: {tokens_per_future_traj}, Got: {n_valid_tokens[idx].item()}.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n            )\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Only gather from positions where we have valid tokens and within our output size\\\\\\\\n    cumsum_indices = torch.cumsum(valid_mask.int(), dim=1) - 1\\\\\\\\n    output_mask = valid_mask &amp; (cumsum_indices &lt; tokens_per_future_traj)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    if output_mask.any():\\\\\\\\n        batch_indices = torch.arange(batch_size, device=device).unsqueeze(1).expand(-1, seq_len)\\\\\\\\n        output_positions = cumsum_indices[output_mask]\\\\\\\\n        batch_ids = batch_indices[output_mask]\\\\\\\\n        token_values = extracted_tokens[output_mask]\\\\\\\\n        token_values = token_values - future_token_start_idx\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Check for invalid tokens\\\\\\\\n        invalid_tokens = (token_values &lt; 0) | (token_values &gt; traj_tokenizer_vocab_size)\\\\\\\\n        if invalid_tokens.any():\\\\\\\\n            logger.warning(f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Invalid token ids found in {invalid_tokens.sum().item()} positions.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Clamp to valid range\\\\\\\\n        token_values = torch.clamp(token_values, min=0, max=traj_tokenizer_vocab_size - 1)\\\\\\\\n        traj_tokens[batch_ids, output_positions] = token_values\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    return traj_tokens\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ndef extract_between_special_tokens(decoded_batch: list[str], token: str) -&gt; list[str]:\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Extract text between special tokens.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Args:\\\\\\\\n        decoded_batch (list[str]): the data we extract from\\\\\\\\n        token (str): the special token we are interested\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Returns:\\\\\\\\n        list: the extracted string\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    start_token = to_special_token(f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{token}_start\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n    end_token = to_special_token(f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{token}_end\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    out: list[str] = []\\\\\\\\n    apnd = out.append\\\\\\\\n    for s in decoded_batch:\\\\\\\\n        before_end, sep, _ = s.partition(end_token)\\\\\\\\n        if not sep:\\\\\\\\n            apnd(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n            continue\\\\\\\\n        i = before_end.rfind(start_token)\\\\\\\\n        if i != -1:\\\\\\\\n            apnd(before_end[i + len(start_token) :].strip())\\\\\\\\n        else:\\\\\\\\n            apnd(before_end.strip())\\\\\\\\n    return out\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ndef extract_text_tokens(\\\\\\\\n    tokenizer: AutoTokenizer, output_tokens: torch.Tensor\\\\\\\\n) -&gt; dict[str, list[str]]:\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Extract text tokens (e.g., cot, meta_action, question, answers) from the output tokens.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Args:\\\\\\\\n        output_tokens (torch.Tensor): The output tokens of shape [B*ns*nj, L].\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Returns:\\\\\\\\n        dict[str, list[str]]: A dict containing all text data.\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    # decode the batch of tokens into strings\\\\\\\\n    decoded_batch = tokenizer.batch_decode(output_tokens, skip_special_tokens=False)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    extract_tokens = [\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"cot\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"meta_action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"answer\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]\\\\\\\\n    extracted_text = {}\\\\\\\\n    for token in extract_tokens:\\\\\\\\n        extracted_text[token] = extract_between_special_tokens(decoded_batch, token)\\\\\\\\n    return extracted_text\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nclass StopAfterEOS(StoppingCriteria):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Stopping criteria that stops the generation after one more token is generated\\\\\\\\n    after the first EOS token is generated.\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(self, eos_token_id: int):\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Args:\\\\\\\\n        eos_token_id (int): The EOS token ID.\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        self.eos_token_id = eos_token_id\\\\\\\\n        self.eos_found = None\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def __call__(self, input_ids: torch.LongTensor, scores: torch.FloatTensor, **kwargs) -&gt; bool:\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Call the stopping criteria.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            input_ids (torch.LongTensor): The input IDs of shape [B, L].\\\\\\\\n            scores (torch.FloatTensor): The scores of shape [B, L, vocab_size].\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Returns:\\\\\\\\n            bool: Whether to stop the generation.\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        batch_size = input_ids.shape[0]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Initialize tracking on first call\\\\\\\\n        if self.eos_found is None:\\\\\\\\n            self.eos_found = torch.zeros(batch_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=input_ids.device)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        if self.eos_found.all():\\\\\\\\n            return True\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Check which sequences have EOS in the last generated token\\\\\\\\n        last_tokens = input_ids[:, -1]\\\\\\\\n        current_has_eos = last_tokens == self.eos_token_id\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Update which sequences just found EOS\\\\\\\\n        self.eos_found = self.eos_found | current_has_eos\\\\\\\\n        return False\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ndef replace_padding_after_eos(\\\\\\\\n    token_ids: torch.Tensor,\\\\\\\\n    eos_token_id: int,\\\\\\\\n    pad_token_id: int,\\\\\\\\n) -&gt; torch.Tensor:\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Overwrite token IDs after the first EOS token with padding token ID.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Args:\\\\\\\\n        token_ids (torch.Tensor): Token IDs of shape [B, L].\\\\\\\\n        eos_token_id (int): The end-of-sequence token ID to search for.\\\\\\\\n        pad_token_id (int): The padding token ID to use for masking.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Returns:\\\\\\\\n        torch.Tensor: Token IDs with padding after the first EOS token of shape [B, L].\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Examples:\\\\\\\\n        &gt;&gt;&gt; token_ids = torch.tensor([[1, 2, 3, 0, 5, 6], [7, 8, 0, 9, 0, 10]])\\\\\\\\n        &gt;&gt;&gt; mask_after_eos(token_ids, eos_token_id=0, pad_token_id=-100)\\\\\\\\n        tensor([[   1,    2,    3,    0, -100, -100],\\\\\\\\n                [   7,    8,    0, -100, -100, -100]])\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    batch_size, seq_len = token_ids.shape\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Find positions of EOS tokens\\\\\\\\n    eos_mask = token_ids == eos_token_id  # [B, L]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Get the position of the first EOS token in each sequence\\\\\\\\n    # Add seq_len where there's no EOS to handle sequences without EOS\\\\\\\\n    eos_positions = torch.where(\\\\\\\\n        eos_mask,\\\\\\\\n        torch.arange(seq_len, device=token_ids.device).unsqueeze(0).expand(batch_size, -1),\\\\\\\\n        torch.tensor(seq_len, device=token_ids.device),\\\\\\\\n    )\\\\\\\\n    first_eos_pos = eos_positions.min(dim=1, keepdim=True)[0]  # [B, 1]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Create a mask for positions after the first EOS\\\\\\\\n    position_indices = torch.arange(seq_len, device=token_ids.device).unsqueeze(0)  # [1, L]\\\\\\\\n    mask_after = position_indices &gt; first_eos_pos  # [B, L]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    # Apply padding inplace\\\\\\\\n    token_ids[mask_after] = pad_token_id\\\\\\\\n    return All rights reserved.\\\\\\\\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\\\\\\\n#\\\\\\\\n# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"License\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\\\\\\\n# You may obtain a copy of the License at\\\\\\\\n#\\\\\\\\n# http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\\\\\\\n#\\\\\\\\n# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\\\\\\\n# distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"AS IS\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" BASIS,\\\\\\\\n# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\\\\\\\n# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\\\\\\\n# limitations under the License.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport einops\\\\\\\\nimport numpy as np\\\\\\\\nimport torch\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nclass DeltaTrajectoryTokenizer:\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Delta trajectory tokenizers.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\n        ego_xyz_min: tuple[float, float, float] = (-4, -4, -10),\\\\\\\\n        ego_xyz_max: tuple[float, float, float] = (4, 4, 10),\\\\\\\\n        ego_yaw_min: float = -np.pi,\\\\\\\\n        ego_yaw_max: float = np.pi,\\\\\\\\n        num_bins: int = 1000,\\\\\\\\n        predict_yaw: bool = False,\\\\\\\\n        load_weights: bool = False,\\\\\\\\n    ):\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Initializes the tokenizer.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        self.ego_xyz_min = ego_xyz_min\\\\\\\\n        self.ego_xyz_max = ego_xyz_max\\\\\\\\n        self.num_bins = num_bins\\\\\\\\n        self._predict_yaw = predict_yaw\\\\\\\\n        self.ego_yaw_min = ego_yaw_min\\\\\\\\n        self.ego_yaw_max = ego_yaw_max\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    @property\\\\\\\\n    def vocab_size(self) -&gt; int:\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Tokens are integers from the set {0, 1, ..., vocab_size - 1}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        return self.num_bins\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def encode(\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\n        hist_xyz: torch.Tensor,\\\\\\\\n        hist_rot: torch.Tensor,\\\\\\\\n        fut_xyz: torch.Tensor,\\\\\\\\n        fut_rot: torch.Tensor,\\\\\\\\n        hist_tstamp: torch.Tensor | None = None,\\\\\\\\n        fut_tstamp: torch.Tensor | None = None,\\\\\\\\n    ) -&gt; torch.LongTensor:\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Encodes the trajectories as discrete tokens. The model conditions on the historical\\\\\\\\n        waypoints to tokenize the future waypoints. Trajectories can be provided in any coordinate\\\\\\\\n        frame. Timestamps can be provided with any time-origin.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            hist_xyz (torch.Tensor): Historical locations XYZ. Shape: (B, Th, 3).\\\\\\\\n            hist_rot (torch.Tensor): Historical rotations. Shape: (B, Th, 3, 3).\\\\\\\\n            fut_xyz (torch.Tensor): Future locations XYZ. Shape: (B, Tf, 3).\\\\\\\\n            fut_rot (torch.Tensor): Future rotations. Shape: (B, Tf, 3, 3).\\\\\\\\n            hist_tstamp (torch.Tensor): Historical time stamps. Shape: (B, Th).\\\\\\\\n            fut_tstamp (torch.Tensor): Future time stamps. Shape: (B, Tf).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Returns:\\\\\\\\n            torch.LongTensor: The token indices. Shape: (B, num_tokens_per_trajectory).\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        del hist_xyz, hist_rot, hist_tstamp, fut_tstamp\\\\\\\\n        xyz = torch.nn.functional.pad(fut_xyz, [0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0])\\\\\\\\n        xyz = xyz[:, 1:] - xyz[:, :-1]\\\\\\\\n        ego_xyz_max = torch.tensor(self.ego_xyz_max, dtype=xyz.dtype, device=xyz.device)\\\\\\\\n        ego_xyz_min = torch.tensor(self.ego_xyz_min, dtype=xyz.dtype, device=xyz.device)\\\\\\\\n        xyz = (xyz - ego_xyz_min) / (ego_xyz_max - ego_xyz_min)\\\\\\\\n        xyz = (xyz * (self.num_bins - 1)).round().long()\\\\\\\\n        xyz = xyz.clamp(0, self.num_bins - 1)\\\\\\\\n        if not self._predict_yaw:\\\\\\\\n            return einops.rearrange(xyz, \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b n m -&gt; b (n m)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n        # Extract yaw angles from rotation matrices\\\\\\\\n        yaw = torch.atan2(fut_rot[..., 0, 1], fut_rot[..., 0, 0])\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Calculate delta yaw\\\\\\\\n        yaw_padded = torch.nn.functional.pad(yaw, [1, 0, 0, 0])\\\\\\\\n        delta_yaw = yaw_padded[:, 1:] - yaw_padded[:, :-1]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Normalize delta yaw to [-pi, pi]\\\\\\\\n        delta_yaw = torch.atan2(torch.sin(delta_yaw), torch.cos(delta_yaw))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Scale and quantize delta yaw\\\\\\\\n        delta_yaw = (delta_yaw - self.ego_yaw_min) / (self.ego_yaw_max - self.ego_yaw_min)\\\\\\\\n        delta_yaw = (delta_yaw * (self.num_bins - 1)).round().long()\\\\\\\\n        delta_yaw = delta_yaw.clamp(0, self.num_bins - 1)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        xyzw = torch.cat([xyz, delta_yaw.unsqueeze(-1)], dim=-1)  # Shape: (B, Tf, 4)\\\\\\\\n        return einops.rearrange(xyzw, \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b n m -&gt; b (n m)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    def decode(\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\n        hist_xyz: torch.Tensor,\\\\\\\\n        hist_rot: torch.Tensor,\\\\\\\\n        tokens: torch.LongTensor,\\\\\\\\n        hist_tstamp: torch.Tensor | None = None,\\\\\\\\n    ) -&gt; tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor | None]:\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Decodes the given tokens into future trajectories. The future trajectory is returned in\\\\\\\\n        the same coordinate frame as the historical trajectory. Timestamps can be provided with any\\\\\\\\n        time-origin.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Args:\\\\\\\\n            hist_xyz (torch.Tensor): Historical locations XYZ. Shape: (B, Th, 3).\\\\\\\\n            hist_rot (torch.Tensor): Historical rotations. Shape: (B, Th, 3, 3).\\\\\\\\n            tokens (torch.LongTensor): The token indices. Shape: (B, num_tokens_per_trajectory).\\\\\\\\n            hist_tstamp (torch.Tensor): Historical time stamps. Shape: (B, Th).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Returns:\\\\\\\\n            fut_xyz (torch.Tensor): Future locations XYZ. Shape: (B, Tf, 3).\\\\\\\\n            fut_rot (torch.Tensor): Future rotations. Shape: (B, Tf, 3, 3).\\\\\\\\n            fut_tstamp (torch.Tensor): Future time stamps. Shape: (B, Tf).\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n        del hist_tstamp\\\\\\\\n        m = 4 if self._predict_yaw else 3\\\\\\\\n        xyzw = einops.rearrange(tokens, \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b (n m) -&gt; b n m\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", m=m).to(hist_xyz.dtype)\\\\\\\\n        xyz = xyzw[..., :3]\\\\\\\\n        xyz = xyz / (self.num_bins - 1)\\\\\\\\n        ego_xyz_max = torch.tensor(self.ego_xyz_max, dtype=xyz.dtype, device=xyz.device)\\\\\\\\n        ego_xyz_min = torch.tensor(self.ego_xyz_min, dtype=xyz.dtype, device=xyz.device)\\\\\\\\n        xyz = xyz * (ego_xyz_max - ego_xyz_min) + ego_xyz_min\\\\\\\\n        fut_xyz = torch.cumsum(xyz, dim=1)\\\\\\\\n        if not self._predict_yaw:\\\\\\\\n            xyz_cpu = fut_xyz.cpu().numpy().astype(float)\\\\\\\\n            fut_rot = get_yaw_rotation_matrices(xyz_cpu)\\\\\\\\n            fut_rot = torch.tensor(fut_rot, device=fut_xyz.device, dtype=fut_xyz.dtype)\\\\\\\\n            return fut_xyz, fut_rot, None\\\\\\\\n        yaw_tokens = xyzw[..., 3]\\\\\\\\n        yaw = yaw_tokens.float() / (self.num_bins - 1)\\\\\\\\n        yaw = yaw * (self.ego_yaw_max - self.ego_yaw_min) + self.ego_yaw_min\\\\\\\\n        yaw = torch.cumsum(yaw, dim=1)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        # Convert yaw angles to rotation matrices\\\\\\\\n        cos_yaw = torch.cos(yaw)\\\\\\\\n        sin_yaw = torch.sin(yaw)\\\\\\\\n        zeros = torch.zeros_like(cos_yaw)\\\\\\\\n        ones = torch.ones_like(cos_yaw)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        fut_rot = torch.stack(\\\\\\\\n            [\\\\\\\\n                torch.stack([cos_yaw, -sin_yaw, zeros], dim=-1),\\\\\\\\n                torch.stack([sin_yaw, cos_yaw, zeros], dim=-1),\\\\\\\\n                torch.stack([zeros, zeros, ones], dim=-1),\\\\\\\\n            ],\\\\\\\\n            dim=-2,\\\\\\\\n        ).to(device=hist_rot.device, dtype=hist_rot.dtype)\\\\\\\\n        return fut_xyz, fut_rot, None\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\ndef get_yaw_rotation_matrices(trajectory, window_size=10, poly_order=3):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Calculate yaw rotation matrices using polynomial fitting for both x(t) and y(t)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Args:\\\\\\\\n        trajectory: np.array of shape (B, N, 3) for batch of x,y,z coordinates\\\\\\\\n        window_size: size of window for polynomial fitting\\\\\\\\n        poly_order: order of polynomial to fit\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    Returns:\\\\\\\\n        rotation_matrices: rotation matrices at each point, shape (B, N, 3, 3)\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    B, N = trajectory.shape[:2]\\\\\\\\n    rotation_matrices = []\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    for b in range(B):\\\\\\\\n        traj_batch = trajectory[b]  # (N, 3)\\\\\\\\n        batch_matrices = []\\\\\\\\n        batch_yaws = []\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        for i in range(N):\\\\\\\\n            # Get window indices with padding for edges\\\\\\\\n            start_idx = max(0, i - window_size // 2)\\\\\\\\n            end_idx = min(N, start_idx + window_size)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            # Adjust window if at edges\\\\\\\\n            if end_idx - start_idx &lt; window_size:\\\\\\\\n                start_idx = max(0, end_idx - window_size)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            # Get points in window\\\\\\\\n            window_points = traj_batch[start_idx:end_idx]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            # Use time parameter t\\\\\\\\n            t = np.arange(len(window_points))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            # Fit polynomials to both x(t) and y(t)\\\\\\\\n            x_coeffs = np.polyfit(t, window_points[:, 0], poly_order)\\\\\\\\n            y_coeffs = np.polyfit(t, window_points[:, 1], poly_order)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            # Calculate derivatives at center point\\\\\\\\n            center_t = min(i - start_idx, window_size - 1)\\\\\\\\n            x_deriv = np.polyder(x_coeffs)\\\\\\\\n            y_deriv = np.polyder(y_coeffs)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            dx = np.polyval(x_deriv, center_t)\\\\\\\\n            dy = np.polyval(y_deriv, center_t)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            # Calculate yaw angle from dx, dy\\\\\\\\n            yaw = np.arctan2(dy, dx)\\\\\\\\n            batch_yaws.append(yaw)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            # Create 3x3 rotation matrix for yaw\\\\\\\\n            cos_yaw = np.cos(yaw)\\\\\\\\n            sin_yaw = np.sin(yaw)\\\\\\\\n            rotation_matrix = np.array([[cos_yaw, -sin_yaw, 0], [sin_yaw, cos_yaw, 0], [0, 0, 1]])\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            batch_matrices.append(rotation_matrix)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        rotation_matrices.append(batch_matrices)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    return np.array(rotation_matrices)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":217}\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"3acbb0d0-156b-4e7f-bf7f-5f244c19fae2\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"f304e565-6054-4b13-bd23-07e01bba8b15\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-04T19:01:04.342Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[], ngBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-04T19:01:04.344Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_9c8ed558-cee4-42d5-8f27-588c85e8612\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_9c8ed558-cee4-42d5-8f27-588c85e8612\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/src/alpamayo_r1/action_space/discrete_action_space.py\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/src/alpamayo_r1/action_space/discrete_action_space.py\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/data/Zhengyang/alpamayo/src/alpamayo_r1/action_space/discrete_action_space.py\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"#
